---
title: "ACTG - Prior analysis"
header-includes:
   - \usepackage{bm}
output: pdf_document
---


We have 

\begin{align*}
  p(m \mid D) &= \frac{Z_m(D) p(m)}{\sum_{j \in \mathcal{M}} Z_j(D) p(j)},
  \\
  &= \frac{Z_m(D) \frac{Z_j(D_0)}{\sum_{k \in \mathcal{M}} Z_k(D_0)} }
  {\sum_{j \in \mathcal{M}} Z_j(D) \frac{Z_m(D_0)}{\sum_{k \in \mathcal{M}} Z_k(D_0)} }, 
  \\
  &= \frac{Z_m(D) Z_m(D_0) }{\sum_{j \in \mathcal{M}} Z_j(D) Z_j(D_0) } ,
  \\
  &= \frac{\int L(\beta^{(m)} | D) \pi\left(\beta^{(m)}, a_0 \mid D_0\right) d\beta^{(m)} da_0 \int L({\beta}^{(m)} | D_0) \pi_0 \left({\beta}^{(m)} \mid \psi_0 \right) d\beta^{(m)} }{\sum_{j \in \mathcal{M}} \int L({\beta}^{(j)} | D) \pi\left(\beta^{(j)}, a_0 \mid D_0\right) d\beta^{(j)} da_0 
  \int L({\beta}^{(j)} | D_0) \pi_0 \left({\beta}^{(j)} \mid \psi_0 \right) d\beta^{(j)} },
  \\
  &= \frac{\int L(\beta^{(m)} | D) \frac{L\left(\beta^{(m)} \mid D_0\right)^{a_0}}{C(a_0)} \pi_A(a_0) \pi_0\left(\beta^{(m)} \mid \tau_0\right) d\beta^{(m)} da_0 \int L({\beta}^{(m)} | D_0) \pi_0 \left({\beta}^{(m)} \mid \psi_0 \right) d\beta^{(m)} }{\sum_{j \in \mathcal{M}} \int L({\beta}^{(j)} | D) \frac{L\left(\beta^{(m)} \mid D_0\right)^{a_0}}{C(a_0)} \pi_A(a_0) \pi_0\left(\beta^{(m)} \mid \tau_0\right) d\beta^{(j)} da_0 
  \int L({\beta}^{(j)} | D_0) \pi_0 \left({\beta}^{(j)} \mid \psi_0 \right) d\beta^{(j)} },
\end{align*}

where $a_0 \sim \text{Beta}(\delta_0, \lambda_0)$, $\boldsymbol{\beta}^{(m)} \mid \psi_0 \sim N(\bm{0}, \phi_0^2 \cdot \boldsymbol{I}_{p^{(m)}})$ and $\boldsymbol{\beta}^{(m)} \mid \tau_0 \sim N(\bm{0}, \tau_0^2 \cdot \boldsymbol{I}_{p^{(m)}})$.

# Prior sensitivity

We start by computing probabilities for several values of $\psi_0$, $(\delta_0, \lambda_0) = (1,1)$ and $\tau_0 = \sqrt{5}$:


\begin{table}[ht]
\centering
\begin{tabular}{crrrr}
  \hline
 $m$ & $p(m)$ & $\log(p(D | m))$ & $p(m | D)$ & $\psi_0$ \\ 
  \hline
  age, treatment, race, cd4 & 0.802 & -35.854 & 0.815 & 0.250 \\ 
  age, treatment, race, cd4 & 0.554 & -35.876 & 0.552 & 0.500 \\ 
  age, treatment, cd4 & 0.487 & -35.869 & 0.488 & 1.000 \\ 
  age, treatment, cd4 & 0.295 & -35.846 & 0.303 & 2.000 \\ 
   \hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

<!-- ```{r psi0, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="Posterior probability of the best model for several values of $\\psi_0$.", out.width = '110%'} -->
<!-- knitr::include_graphics("figures/best_models_psi0.png") -->
<!-- ``` -->

Then, we compute probabilities for several values of $\tau_0$, $(\delta_0, \lambda_0) = (1,1)$ and $\psi_0 = \sqrt{5}$:

\begin{table}[!ht]
\centering
\begin{tabular}{crrrr}
  \hline
  $m$ & $p(m)$ & $\log(Z_m(D))$ & $p(m \mid D)$ & $\tau_0$ \\ 
  \hline
  age, treatment, race, cd4 & 0.286 & -39.365 & 0.497 & 0.250 \\ 
  age, treatment, cd4 & 0.487 & -36.665 & 0.477 & 0.500 \\ 
  age, treatment, cd4 & 0.486 & -35.366 & 0.499 & 1.000 \\ 
  age, treatment, cd4 & 0.486 & -34.956 & 0.518 & 2.000 \\ 
   \hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

<!-- ```{r tau0, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="Posterior probability of the best model for several values of $\\tau_0$.", out.width = '110%'} -->
<!-- knitr::include_graphics("figures/best_models_tau0.png") -->
<!-- ``` -->

Lastly, we compute probabilities for several values of $(\delta_0, \lambda_0)$ and $\psi_0 = \tau_0 = \sqrt{5}$:

\begin{table}[ht]
\centering
\begin{tabular}{crrrr}
  \hline
  $m$ & $p(m)$ & $\log(Z_m(D))$ & $p(m \mid D)$ & $(\delta_0, \lambda_0)$ \\  
  \hline
  age, treatment, cd4 &  0.487 & -35.856 & 0.491 & $(1, 1)$ \\
  age, treatment, cd4 &  0.486 & -35.806 & 0.490 & $(2, 2)$ \\
  age, treatment, cd4 &  0.487 & -37.556 & 0.442 & $(1, 10)$ \\
  age, treatment, cd4 &  0.487 & -35.429 & 0.497 & $(10, 1)$ \\ 
   \hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

```{r psi0, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="ATE distribution with normal prior for several values of $\\psi_0$.", out.width = '50%', fig.align='center'}
knitr::include_graphics("figures/ate_psi0.png")
```

# Prior comparison

Now, we set $\beta_0^{(m)} \sim \text{Cauchy(0, 10)}$ and $\beta_j^{(m)} \sim \text{Cauchy(0, 2.5)}$ for $j = 1, \ldots, p^{(m)}-1$ with $a_0 \sim \text{Beta}(1, 1)$.

\begin{table}[ht]
\centering
\begin{tabular}{crrr}
  \hline
 $m$ & $p(m)$ & $\log(Z_m(D))$ & $p(m \mid D)$ \\ 
  \hline
  age, treatment, cd4 & 0.196 & -34.885 & 0.253 \\ 
  age, treatment, race, cd4 & 0.276 & -35.509 & 0.191 \\ 
  treatment, cd4 & 0.085 & -34.776 & 0.123 \\ 
  treatment, race, cd4 & 0.140 & -35.357 & 0.113 \\ 
  age, race, cd4 & 0.131 & -35.414 & 0.100 \\ 
  age, cd4 & 0.066 & -34.746 & 0.099 \\ 
  race, cd4 & 0.074 & -35.226 & 0.068 \\ 
  cd4 & 0.031 & -34.577 & 0.054 \\ 
  age, treatment & 0.000 & -41.405 & 0.000 \\ 
  age, treatment, race & 0.000 & -42.075 & 0.000 \\ 
  age & 0.000 & -41.591 & 0.000 \\ 
  treatment & 0.000 & -41.758 & 0.000 \\ 
  age, race & 0.000 & -42.320 & 0.000 \\ 
  treatment, race & 0.000 & -42.392 & 0.000 \\ 
  race & 0.000 & -42.558 & 0.000 \\ 
   \hline
\end{tabular}
\end{table}

```{r cauchy, echo=FALSE, fig.cap="ATE distribution with Cauchy prior.", out.width = '90%', fig.align='center'}
knitr::include_graphics("figures/ate_cauchy.png")
